title: acer truncatum subsp. mayrii
intro: Acer truncatum subsp. mayrii, also known as the Mayri's maple or Mayri's paperbark maple, is a subspecies of the Acer truncatum tree native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Acer truncatum subsp. mayrii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Acer truncatum subsp. mayrii can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions.
